notes: Grade 8 has distinct testing from grades 6 and 7: in public/charter/virtual the FL Statewide Science Assessment is given in grades 5 and 8 (NOT 6 or 7) — an 8th-grade science accountability point. Verify current administration on the FLDOE assessment schedule.

Grade 8 still takes FAST ELA Reading and FAST Mathematics; an 8th grader enrolled in a high-school-credit course (e.g., Algebra 1, Geometry, Biology 1) takes that course's EOC instead of the grade-level math, which can earn high-school credit. Verify the student's course enrollment.
Use the individual grade (8) — not just the 6-8 band — when selecting B.E.S.T./NGSSS grade-8 standards, since 8th-grade expectations differ from 6th/7th.
